summary: Advanced the sysdolphin baselib sislib decompilation by replacing large dead-register/#if-0 scaffolding with structured C for SIS text encoding, formatted text insertion/replacement, text-state stack save/restore, and SIS render parsing. The PR added a real implementation for HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, rewrote HSD_SisLib_803A67EC and HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 into clearer buffer/encoded-byte logic, split HSD_SisLib_803A7684 out as a typed helper, improved HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C and HSD_SisLib_803A8134, and refined HSD_SisLib_803A84BC call sites. decomp.dev reported seven item-match improvements in main/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib, including HSD_SisLib_803A7684 from 0.00% to 55.05%, HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 from 0.00% to 78.79%, HSD_SisLib_803A84BC from 66.31% to 89.15%, and HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C from 67.41% to 97.87%.

key_files.role: Main sislib decompilation work: rewrote encoding, insertion/replacement, state-stack, measurement, and render parser logic.
Updated HSD_SisLib_803A7684 prototype from UNK_T/u8/u8 to HSD_Text*/u8*/u8.

what_changed: Updated todo comments for HSD_SisLib_803A5798 and HSD_SisLib_803A594C to document remaining register-allocation, scheduling, and literal-pooling issues rather than treating them as unknown broad mismatches.

Reworked HSD_SisLib_803A67EC from goto-heavy/dead-register scaffolding into a for-loop encoder that converts ASCII-like input to SIS encoded byte pairs using Shift-JIS high/low byte selection and lookup tables lbl_8040C8C0 and HSD_SisLib_8040C680.

Reworked HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 to use local buffer[256] and encoded[128], call vsnprintf plus HSD_SisLib_803A67EC, grow the SIS buffer in 0x80-byte increments through HSD_SisLib_803A5798/HSD_SisLib_803A594C, emit position/color/scale control bytes through a data_0 cursor, bulk-copy encoded bytes in 8-byte chunks plus tail bytes, and return ((sisLib_803A7664_t*) alloc)->xC++.

Added HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, which finds an existing SIS entry with fn_803A6FEC, formats and encodes replacement text, grows or shrinks the data segment as needed, shifts the trailing bytes forward or backward, writes the new encoded payload, terminates it with 0xF, and returns success/failure.

Changed HSD_SisLib_803A8134 to call HSD_SisLib_803A7684 with a cursor pointer rather than the byte value at the cursor, improved typed reads for s16/u16 control values, and replaced a clear-loop goto with a while loop.

Replaced a static inline HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C_inline helper plus wrapper with a direct HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C implementation that scans text->string_buffer as bytes, restores spacing/color/scale/alignment/value state based on flags, removes matched stack entries, compacts remaining bytes, and zero-fills the tail.

Implemented HSD_SisLib_803A7684 as a non-inline typed function taking HSD_Text*, u8* cursor, and u8 flags. It saves current text state entries into text->string_buffer, grows that buffer by 0x10 when needed, copies old bytes in 8-byte chunks plus tail bytes, zero-fills the new capacity, and writes entries for spacing, active color, scale, alignment, or value depending on flags.

Adjusted HSD_SisLib_803A84BC to use the typed HSD_SisLib_803A7684 calls, call HSD_SisLib_803A8134 for line measurement in case 7, add a line_height_out local, and perform more accurate s16/u16 cursor-based control reads.
Updated sislib.h declaration for HSD_SisLib_803A7684 from void HSD_SisLib_803A7684(UNK_T, u8, u8) to void HSD_SisLib_803A7684(HSD_Text*, u8*, u8).
smart_moves: Converted many fake dead_r*/dead_sp* variables and #if 0 pseudo-code blocks into direct byte-buffer operations while preserving matching-sensitive manual copy loops.
Kept compiler-shaped 8-byte bulk-copy loops followed by scalar tail loops instead of replacing them with memcpy, which is important for matching old Metrowerks output.

Used pointer-to-cursor style writes, including u8** cur = (u8**) &alloc->data_0 in HSD_SisLib_803A6B98, to model code that advances an embedded buffer cursor while updating the owning allocation structure.
Documented remaining allocator mismatches precisely as register allocation/scheduling and literal-pooling issues, making future matching work easier to triage.

Improved function typing at the header boundary for HSD_SisLib_803A7684, which allowed call sites to pass the SIS cursor pointer and use proper HSD_Text state instead of opaque UNK_T and incorrect u8 arguments.

Handled SIS text replacement in HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 in both growth and shrink directions, including reallocating the containing SIS buffer only when required and recomputing playhead after relocation.
decomp_lessons: For SIS/text bytecode code, byte-pointer types are often more truthful and more match-friendly than treating buffers as s32* or struct-like fake fields.

When a function appends to or rewrites variable-length byte streams, preserve the original manual bulk-copy shape: old compiler output may use explicit 8-byte unrolled copy loops plus residual-byte loops.
A helper prototype can materially affect matching and correctness. Passing u8* cursor to HSD_SisLib_803A7684 fixed call sites that had been passing *cursor as a byte.
Do not remove apparent redundant buffer-growth/copy/zero-fill code too aggressively; it may be modeling a state stack used by text layout and render parsing.
Explicitly naming remaining diffs as regalloc, scheduling, reassociation, or literal-pooling issues is useful when the C is semantically close but not exactly matching.
Replacing fake inline helpers with one direct function can improve match quality when the original binary did not preserve the same abstraction boundary.
naming_conventions: Retained address-based names for unknown/non-symbolized functions: HSD_SisLib_803A67EC, HSD_SisLib_803A6B98, HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, HSD_SisLib_803A7684, HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C, HSD_SisLib_803A8134, HSD_SisLib_803A84BC.

Used descriptive local names for byte-stream work: buffer, encoded, playhead, cursor, old_buf, new_buf, copy_src, copy_dst, bulk_src, tail_src, tail_count, required_size, encoded_len, old_size, new_size.
Used state-oriented names for SIS encoding: has_kerning, state_dot, state_colon, state_digit, out_idx, in_idx, lut_ptr, lut_idx, sjis_hi, sjis_lo.
Used text-state names already present in HSD_Text fields: string_buffer, sis_buffer, active_color, text_color, alignment, x6C, x6E, x78, x80.
Header conventions prefer typed parameters once known: HSD_Text* and u8* replaced UNK_T and raw u8 for HSD_SisLib_803A7684.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Modeled register/scheduling-sensitive allocator functions with comments rather than overfitting unrelated logic: HSD_SisLib_803A5798 remained around 99.40% with regalloc/literal-pooling notes; HSD_SisLib_803A594C around 92.57% with coalesce-block scheduling notes.
Used explicit pointer arithmetic and casts, such as *(s16*) (cursor + 1), *(u16*) (cursor + 3), and (u8*) HSD_SisLib_8040C680 + lut_idx * 2, to match bytecode parsing patterns.
Preserved do/while loops with decrementing bulk_count and separate tail loops for encoded-byte copies and buffer moves.
For growth in HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, shifted trailing bytes backward from the end using negative indexing so overlapping moves behave like the original handwritten memmove.
For shrinking in HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, shifted trailing bytes forward from playhead + shrink_diff and then adjusted alloc->data_0 backward.
Used fixed-size stack buffers buffer[256] and encoded[128] for formatted text encoding rather than heap allocation.
Maintained 0x80-byte allocation growth for SIS data buffers and 0x10-byte growth for text->string_buffer state stack.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported 7 improvements in unmatched items for main/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib: HSD_SisLib_803A7684 0.00%→55.05%, HSD_SisLib_803A84BC 66.31%→89.15%, HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 0.00%→78.79%, HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 51.49%→87.35%, HSD_SisLib_803A67EC 35.58%→60.71%, HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C 67.41%→97.87%, and HSD_SisLib_803A8134 82.88%→94.84%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish matching HSD_SisLib_803A5798 literal pooling or HSD_SisLib_803A594C coalesce-block regalloc.
Search for subsequent renames or struct recovery around sislib_UnkAllocData and sisLib_803A7664_t.
Check whether HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, HSD_SisLib_803A7684, and HSD_SisLib_803A84BC received symbol/field naming improvements after this PR.
Compare later sislib.c changes for replacement of address-based HSD_SisLib_* names with semantic names.
